question: Is the ~10 A anchor offset a rigid-receptor artifact or a pocket-definition error?
finding: Forcing 5-AMP toward the reference nucleotide position by contracting the box collapses affinity monotonically (-8.8 -> -6.0 -> -3.7 -> +3.8 kcal/mol) and turns positive (steric clash) at 10 A. The apo rigid receptor cannot accommodate the ligand at the exact crystallographic position.
interpretation: Pocket is correctly localized (5-AMP docks well ~10 A away, same Gly-loop/K34/F18 region), but the exact holo nucleotide pose requires receptor flexibility the apo AlphaFold model does not provide. This is an induced-fit / apo-conformation limitation, NOT a site-definition error.
recommended_orthogonal_check: co-folding (Boltz-2) or flexible-loop docking to recover the holo pose
